page: UpCloud DEV-1xCPU-4GB is a Developer family virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor. It features a single shared vCPU mapped to an AMD EPYC 7542 processor operating at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 4.0 GB of system memory and a bundled 40 GB local SSD. Network connectivity is supported by one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks reflect its entry-level, shared-resource design. While single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression achieve average tier results, overall compute and memory bandwidth performance sit in the bottom 10% of tested systems. The single-core Geekbench score is average at 1306.0, but multi-core and specialized workloads, such as software compilation, database operations, and Redis SET operations, show poor performance due to the single shared vCPU limitation.

This server offers high qualitative cost efficiency for non-production environments, providing a functional x86_64 environment with a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio at a low resource density. It is best suited for lightweight workloads such as software development, testing, basic web serving, and running simple utility scripts rather than resource-intensive production applications.
description: UpCloud DEV-1xCPU-4GB is a budget-oriented virtual server in the Developer family, utilizing the KVM hypervisor. It is equipped with a single shared vCPU from an AMD EPYC 7542 processor clocked at 2.0 GHz, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 40 GB of local SSD storage. Benchmark results indicate entry-level performance, with single-core tasks like gzip compression performing at average levels, while multi-core operations, memory bandwidth, and database execution fall into the lower percentiles. This configuration represents a highly cost-efficient option for non-production environments. It is best suited for software development, testing, and lightweight utility hosting.
